PricingBucket.description:
The pricing bucket for which the query is charged at.

For more information on pricing, please visit Amazon Location Service Pricing.

ResultItems.description: List of places or results returned for a query.

PlaceType.description: A PlaceType is a category that the result place must belong to.

Title.description: The localized display name of this result item based on request parameter language.

Label.description: Assembled address value built out of the address components, according to the regional postal rules. This is the correctly formatted address.

Code3.description: Country, represented by its alpha t-character code.

Region.description:
The region or state results should be present in.

Example: North Rhine-Westphalia.

Code.description:
Abbreviated code for a the state, province or region of the country.

Example: BC.

Name.description:
Name for a the state, province, or region of the country.

Example: British Columbia.

SubRegion.description: The sub-region or county for which results should be present in.

Code.description: Abbreviated code for the county or sub-region.

Locality.description:
The city or locality of the address.

Example: Vancouver.

District.description: The district or division of a locality associated with this address.

PostalCode.description: An alphanumeric string included in a postal address to facilitate mail sorting, such as post code, postcode, or ZIP code, for which the result should possess.

Block.description:
Name of the block.

Example: Sunny Mansion 203 block: 2 Chome

Intersection.description:
Name of the streets in the intersection.

Example: ["Friedrichstraße","Unter den Linden"]

Street.description: The name of the street results should be present in.

StreetComponents.description:
Components of the street.

Example: Younge from the "Younge street".

AddressNumber.description: The number that identifies an address within a street.

Building.description: The name of the building at the address.

SecondaryAddressComponents.description: Components that correspond to secondary identifiers on an Address. Secondary address components include information such as Suite or Unit Number, Building, or Floor.

AddressNumberCorrected.description: Boolean indicating if the address provided has been corrected.

PostalCodeDetails.description: Contains details about the postal code of the place/result.

Distance.description: The distance in meters from the QueryPosition.

MapView.description:
The bounding box enclosing the geometric shape (area or line) that an individual result covers.

The bounding box formed is defined as a set 4 coordinates: [{westward lng}, {southern lat}, {eastward lng}, {northern lat}]

Categories.description: Categories of results that results must belong to.

FoodTypes.description: List of food types offered by this result.

AccessPoints.description: Position of the access point represented by longitude and latitude.

TimeZone.description: The time zone in which the place is located.

Offset.description: Time zone offset of the timezone from UTC.

OffsetSeconds.description: The offset of the time zone from UTC, in seconds.

PoliticalView.description: The alpha-2 or alpha-3 character code for the political view of a country. The political view applies to the results of the request to represent unresolved territorial claims through the point of view of the specified country.

MatchScores.description: Indicates how well the entire input matches the returned. It is equal to 1 if all input tokens are recognized and matched.

Overall.description: Indicates how well the entire input matches the returned. It is equal to 1 if all input tokens are recognized and matched.

Components.description: Indicates how well the component input matches the returned. It is equal to 1 if all input tokens are recognized and matched.

Title.description: Indicates the match score of the title in the text query that match the found title.

Country.description: The alpha-2 or alpha-3 character code for the country that the results will be present in.

Region.description:
The region or state results should be to be present in.

Example: North Rhine-Westphalia.

SubRegion.description: The sub-region or county for which results should be present in.

Locality.description:
The city or locality results should be present in.

Example: Vancouver.

District.description: The district or division of a city the results should be present in.

PostalCode.description: An alphanumeric string included in a postal address to facilitate mail sorting, such as post code, postcode, or ZIP code, for which the result should possess.

Block.description:
Name of the block.

Example: Sunny Mansion 203 block: 2 Chome

Intersection.description:
Name of the streets in the intersection.

Example: ["Friedrichstraße","Unter den Linden"]

AddressNumber.description: The house number or address results should have.

Building.description: The name of the building at the address.

SecondaryAddressComponents.description: Match scores for the secondary address components in the result.

Distance.description: The distance in meters between the center of the search area and this result. Useful to evaluate how far away from the original bias position the result is.

Language.description: A list of BCP 47 compliant language codes for the results to be rendered in. If there is no data for the result in the requested language, data will be returned in the default language for the entry.

PoliticalView.description: The alpha-2 or alpha-3 character code for the political view of a country. The political view applies to the results of the request to represent unresolved territorial claims through the point of view of the specified country.

Highlights.description: Indicates the starting and ending index of the place in the text query that match the found title.

Title.description: Indicates where the title field in the result matches the input query.

Address.description: Describes how part of the result address match the input query.

Label.description: Indicates the starting and ending indexes for result items where they are identified to match the input query. This should be used to provide emphasis to output display to make selecting the correct result from a list easier for end users.

Country.description: The alpha-2 or alpha-3 character code for the country that the results will be present in.

Name.description: Indicates the starting and ending index of the name in the text query that match the found title.

District.description: How to pronounce the district or division of a city results should be present in.

SubDistrict.description: How to pronounce the sub-district or division of a city results should be present in.

Block.description: How to pronounce the name of the block.

SubBlock.description: How to pronounce the name of the sub-block.

Street.description: How to pronounce the name of the street results should be present in.

QueryId.description:
QueryId can be used to complete a follow up query through the SearchText API. The QueryId retains context from the original Suggest request such as filters, political view and language. See the SearchText API documentation for more details SearchText API docs.

The fields QueryText, and QueryID are mutually exclusive.

QueryType.description: The query type. Category queries will search for places which have an entry matching the given category, for example "doctor office". BusinessChain queries will search for instances of a given business.

Highlights.description: Describes how the parts of the response element matched the input query by returning the sections of the response which matched to input query terms.

Title.description: Indicates the starting and ending index of the title in the text query that match the found title.

Label.description: Indicates the starting and ending indexes of the places in the result which were identified to match the textQuery. This result is useful for providing emphasis to results where the user query directly matched to make selecting the correct result from a list easier for an end user.
